3d_model.notes: Massing model shows single-level horizontal volume oriented perpendicular to the Pacific horizon. Main living volume aligned on east-west axis to maximize horizon capture from all principal spaces. Covered terrace extends the interior plane outward with a continuous roofline.

renders.notes: Key perspectives: (1) approach from landside — native scrub integration; (2) horizon view from living/dining; (3) covered terrace-to-pool-deck sequence; (4) overall site massing from aerial.

material_direction.notes: Coastal palette: poured concrete structural frame with locally-sourced stone cladding on wind-facing facades; stucco finish on protected facades; salt-air resistant aluminum window frames throughout; teak and composite decking for terrace and pool deck zones.

color_direction.notes: Neutral base anchored to the sand and scrub palette — warm off-whites, weathered greys, natural wood tones. Interior accents draw from Pacific blues and the green of coastal scrub. Color is restrained to let the horizon read as the dominant visual element.

space_arrangement.notes: All 10 programmed spaces present in preliminary plan. Living/dining (60 sqm) positioned at the horizon axis with full Pacific view. Master suite (45 sqm) at west end with direct terrace access. Pool deck (45 sqm) and covered terrace (55 sqm) form a continuous outdoor living sequence. Studio (18 sqm) positioned at east end with cross-ventilation. Total program: 356 sqm — matches approved area-program.json.

review_notes: Concept is fully aligned with the approved area program (356 sqm, 10 spaces). The horizon-axis organization is the central design driver — all principal spaces are oriented to the Pacific view with the covered terrace acting as a wind-protected transitional zone. No program deviations identified. Recommend approval. Client should note that the coastal zone setback line will be confirmed in Phase 2 once the SEMARNAT permit process begins — the massing concept has been designed with conservative setback assumptions.
